# Review: `deepmind/490.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 490`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.** The module docstring contains only the title, reference link, and a bibliography entry for [Sz76]. There is no plain-English summary of the problem itself. (The theorem docstring has a good description, but the checklist requires one in the module docstring as well.)
- [x] Optional bibliography entry present: `[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.`

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos490 ... end Erdos490`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos490`).
- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements needed; N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def HasDistinctProducts` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] No classical choice or nonconstructive operations; `noncomputable` not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Type (`HasDistinctProducts` returns `Prop`) uses `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] N/A: no functions returning types.
- [x] N/A: no `local notation` needed.
- [x] N/A: optional `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_490` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[Sz76]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a direct assertion ("If ..., then |A|¬∑|B| ‚Č™ N¬≤/log N"), so **Case 3** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for a direct assertion.
- [x] No misplaced quantifiers with `answer()` (not applicable).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved`.
- [x] N/A: not `research open`, so the sorry-free-proof lint check does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5 11`.
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: 5 < 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` in acceptable multi-line form (`... := by\n  sorry`).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] N/A: file is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included (problem is `research solved`, not `research open`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąą`, `‚ąß`, `‚Č§`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**One issue found:**

1. **¬ß3 ‚Äď Module docstring missing plain-English problem description.** The module docstring has the title, reference, and bibliography but lacks a brief description of the problem (e.g., "If A, B ‚äÜ {1,‚Ä¶,N} have all products ab distinct, is it true that |A|¬∑|B| ‚Č™ N¬≤/log N?"). The theorem docstring contains a good description, but the checklist requires one in the module docstring as well.

Everything else conforms to the checklist. The formalization is clean, the custom definition `HasDistinctProducts` is well-documented and correctly captures injectivity of the multiplication map, and the theorem statement is well-structured with proper attributes and docstring.

    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 490
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/490](https://www.erdosproblems.com/490)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üínamespace Erdos490
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/--
    30‚ÜíThe products $ab$ with $a \in A$ and $b \in B$ are all distinct: the multiplication
    31‚Üímap $A \times B \to \mathbb{N}$ is injective.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üídef HasDistinctProducts (A B : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    34‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a‚āĀ ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b‚āĀ ‚ąą B, ‚ąÄ a‚āā ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b‚āā ‚ąą B,
    35‚Üí    a‚āĀ * b‚āĀ = a‚āā * b‚āā ‚Üí a‚āĀ = a‚āā ‚ąß b‚āĀ = b‚āā
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/--
    38‚ÜíIf $A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ are such that all products $ab$ ($a \in A$, $b \in B$)
    39‚Üíare distinct, then $|A| \cdot |B| \ll N^2 / \log N$.
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíFormally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $N$ and all
    42‚Üí$A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ with distinct products,
    43‚Üí$$
    44‚Üí  |A| \cdot |B| \le C \cdot \frac{N^2}{\log N}.
    45‚Üí$$
    46‚Üí
    47‚ÜíProved by Szemer√©di [Sz76].
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    50‚Üítheorem erdos_490 :
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    52‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (N : ‚Ąē) (A B : Finset ‚Ąē),
    53‚Üí        (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
    54‚Üí        (‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, 1 ‚Č§ b ‚ąß b ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí        HasDistinctProducts A B ‚Üí
    56‚Üí        (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) * (B.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * ((N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    57‚Üí  sorry
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üíend Erdos490
    60‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 490

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/490](https://www.erdosproblems.com/490)

[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.
-/

namespace Erdos490

/--
The products $ab$ with $a \in A$ and $b \in B$ are all distinct: the multiplication
map $A \times B \to \mathbb{N}$ is injective.
-/
def HasDistinctProducts (A B : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ a‚āĀ ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b‚āĀ ‚ąą B, ‚ąÄ a‚āā ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b‚āā ‚ąą B,
    a‚āĀ * b‚āĀ = a‚āā * b‚āā ‚Üí a‚āĀ = a‚āā ‚ąß b‚āĀ = b‚āā

/--
If $A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ are such that all products $ab$ ($a \in A$, $b \in B$)
are distinct, then $|A| \cdot |B| \ll N^2 / \log N$.

Formally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $N$ and all
$A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ with distinct products,
$$
  |A| \cdot |B| \le C \cdot \frac{N^2}{\log N}.
$$

Proved by Szemer√©di [Sz76].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_490 :
    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ (N : ‚Ąē) (A B : Finset ‚Ąē),
        (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
        (‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, 1 ‚Č§ b ‚ąß b ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
        HasDistinctProducts A B ‚Üí
        (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) * (B.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * ((N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos490

content.text: The review found one issue: the module docstring is missing a plain-English problem description. Let me fix that.

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 490

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/490](https://www.erdosproblems.com/490)

[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.

input.new_string:
/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 490

If $A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ are such that all products $ab$ ($a \in A$, $b \in B$)
are distinct, is it true that $|A| \cdot |B| \ll N^2 / \log N$?

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/490](https://www.erdosproblems.com/490)

[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 490

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/490](https://www.erdosproblems.com/490)

[Sz76] Szemer√©di, E., *On the number of edges in products of sets of integers*. 1976.
-/

namespace Erdos490

/--
The products $ab$ with $a \in A$ and $b \in B$ are all distinct: the multiplication
map $A \times B \to \mathbb{N}$ is injective.
-/
def HasDistinctProducts (A B : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ a‚āĀ ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b‚āĀ ‚ąą B, ‚ąÄ a‚āā ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b‚āā ‚ąą B,
    a‚āĀ * b‚āĀ = a‚āā * b‚āā ‚Üí a‚āĀ = a‚āā ‚ąß b‚āĀ = b‚āā

/--
If $A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ are such that all products $ab$ ($a \in A$, $b \in B$)
are distinct, then $|A| \cdot |B| \ll N^2 / \log N$.

Formally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $N$ and all
$A, B \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ with distinct products,
$$
  |A| \cdot |B| \le C \cdot \frac{N^2}{\log N}.
$$

Proved by Szemer√©di [Sz76].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_490 :
    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ (N : ‚Ąē) (A B : Finset ‚Ąē),
        (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
        (‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, 1 ‚Č§ b ‚ąß b ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
        HasDistinctProducts A B ‚Üí
        (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) * (B.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * ((N : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / Real.log (N : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos490
